meaning: adj.难看的,不象样的,笨拙的adv.笨拙地,难看地,不雅地
examples: He was a tall, ungainly boy of 18.
Not so long ago, this ungainly partnership was faring poorly.
For the Games it looks ungainly from the outside, with its steel-framed, fabric-sheathed wings of temporary seating.
Its XPS M2010 portable computer is slick, but at 18.3 pounds it's more than a little ungainly.
